executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 103+ organizations compromised in the past 48 hours, spanning critical sectors including public health (Peterborough Public Health), education (Eastern Townships School Board), financial services (National Money Mart - database leaked), and a high-profile consumer brand (Canada Goose). The Qilin ransomware group leads the assault with 22 Canadian victims, followed by Clop (15) and Play (11). Additionally, CCCS has issued an updated advisory on critical Cisco ASA and FTD vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363) affecting network security infrastructure. This represents a mass exploitation event requiring immediate incident response across all affected sectors.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is under severe ransomware attack with 103+ organizations compromised across all sectors in 48 hours. CCCS has issued Update 2 to advisory AL25-012 addressing critical Cisco ASA and FTD vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363). Critical infrastructure targets include public health agencies, municipalities, educational institutions, telecommunications providers, and financial services firms with confirmed database exfiltration.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (Consumer Services) compromised by CoinbaseCartel ransomware group; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) compromised by Sinobi ransomware group
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company (financial services) - database leaked by Everest ransomware group; Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi; Trucash compromised by Lynx; multiple accounting/CPA firms affected including Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, and ReidhurstNagy

report_2w.executive_summary: A massive breach of Canvas LMS by threat actor ShinyHunters has exposed 275 million student records from approximately 9,000 schools and universities across North America, with a ransom deadline of May 12, 2026—during finals week. Multiple critical vulnerabilities are being actively exploited in the wild: Palo Alto Networks firewalls (CVE-2026-0300) have been compromised for several weeks, Ivanti EPMM (CVE-2026-6973) was added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities list, and a Linux kernel privilege escalation flaw (CVE-2026-31431, 'Dirty Frag') enables root access across major distributions. Canadian institutions are directly affected by the Canvas breach, and CCCS has issued four alerts in the past week covering Linux vulnerabilities, cPanel flaws, and social engineering attacks on enterprise SaaS environments.

items.description: Critical RCE vulnerability in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS firewalls
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Zero-day RCE vulnerability in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM)
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Linux kernel 'Dirty Frag' local privilege escalation enabling root access across major distributions
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Vulnerability in cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM)
Vulnerability in ICS systems (Industrial Control Systems)
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: Medium, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Instructure Canvas LMS - approximately 9,000 schools and universities including Harvard, Columbia, Duke, and numerous Canadian institutions; 275 million student records compromised
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact/Extortion), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage Object). Social engineering attacks on SaaS platforms, credential theft, data exfiltration, extortion with public deadline (May 12, 2026)

items.name: RansomHouse
items.targets: Trellix source code repository
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1213 (Data from Information Repositories), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service). Source code theft from cybersecurity vendor

items.name: PCPJack (worm)
items.targets: Cloud infrastructure: Docker, Kubernetes, exposed APIs, MongoDB instances
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1552.001 (Credentials In Files), T1552.004 (Private Keys), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage Object), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact). Credential harvesting (API keys, SSH secrets), worm propagation, removes competing TeamPCP infections

items.name: Handala (Iranian APT)
items.targets: Various targets - continued activity despite regional ceasefires
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter). Iranian state-sponsored espionage operations

items.name: ClickFix campaign operators
items.targets: Australian organizations, macOS users, WordPress sites
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1204.001 (User Execution: Malicious Link), T1027 (Obfuscated Files or Information). Fake CAPTCHA prompts, fake macOS utility lures delivering Vidar Stealer and other infostealers

items.name: Unknown APT (State-sponsored)
items.targets: Palo Alto Networks firewall users
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1133 (External Remote Services). Exploitation of CVE-2026-0300 for several weeks

items.name: TCLBANKER
items.targets: Brazilian banking customers via WhatsApp and Outlook
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1539 (Steal Web Session Cookie). Banking trojan targeting credentials and financial data

items.name: Operation GriefLure (APT)
items.targets: Vietnam military telecom and Philippine healthcare sectors
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1203 (Exploitation for Client Execution), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol). Sophisticated spear phishing campaign

canada_landscape.summary: Canadian institutions are directly impacted by the ShinyHunters Canvas LMS breach affecting thousands of universities and K-12 schools across Canada. CCCS issued four alerts between April 29 and May 8, 2026: AL26-011 covering Linux vulnerabilities CVE-2026-43284 and CVE-2026-43500; AL26-009 (Update 1) for Linux CVE-2026-31431; AL26-010 warning of social engineering attacks on enterprise SaaS environments; and AL26-008 for cPanel/WHM CVE-2026-41940. An Alberta data breach was also reported as a significant scandal.
